contentBlocks.original:
वेदवेदांतवेद्याय मेघश्यामलमूर्तये ।
पुंसां मोहनरूपाय पुण्यश्लोकाय मंगलम् ।।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vedavedāṃtavedyāya meghaśyāmalamūrtaye .
puṃsāṃ mohanarūpāya puṇyaślokāya maṃgalam ..
contentBlocks.translation: O auspicious ruler of Kosala, repository of the magnificent qualities, the off-spring of the emperor (Dasaratha), the universally & dominant Lord, the erudite expert of the Vedas and Vedanta, the dark-grey cloud-complexioned idol, the exquisitely-attractive amongst men, extolled by virtuous hymns, the eternal Parijata, Sri Rama, the depository of all auspicious qualities, the pleasing personality, the amazing reason for the pleasure on the lotus face of Sita - may he shower prosperity eternally.
contentBlocks.meanings: वेदवेदांतवेद्याय (vedavedāṃtavedyāya) – knower of Vedas and Vedanta (veda:Vedas | vedānta:Vedanta | vedya:knower | āya:to); मेघश्यामलमूर्तये (meghaśyāmalamūrtaye) – dark-cloud-complexioned form (megha:cloud | śyāmala:dark | mūrti:form | aye:to); पुंसाम् (puṃsām) – of men; मोहनरूपाय (mohanarūpāya) – enchanting form (mohana:enchanting | rūpa:form | āya:to); पुण्यश्लोकाय (puṇyaślokāya) – to the virtuous hymn (puṇya:virtuous | śloka:hymn | āya:to); मंगलम् (maṃgalam) – auspiciousness;
